data.body:
VALIDATION (3 independent subagents, read-only + empirical). Both prerequisite
bugs REPRODUCED. Most shapes confirmed; the following CORRECTIONS to the plan
above are validated against current code and supersede the originals:

1. CRITICAL — TicketState is produced by TWO DIVERGENT reducers, not one.
   `rebar show` (and the not-found ErrorEnvelope) come from the jq reducer in
   ticket-lib-api.sh (ticket_show), NOT ticket-show.sh. `list`/`search`/native
   `reduce_ticket` use the Python reducer (ticket_reducer/). They are NOT
   byte-identical:
   - jq show: emits conditional `verify_commands`; OMITS `parent_status_uuid`.
   - Python (list/search): always emits `parent_status_uuid`; NEVER emits
     `verify_commands` (no VERIFY_COMMANDS processor exists in _processors.py).
   => The "list/search are just {array of $ref TicketState}" keystone is only
   true for the CORE fields. Action: either (a) make the TicketState schema cover
   BOTH (verify_commands AND parent_status_uuid both optional; additionalProperties
   already true), OR (b) add a PREREQUISITE task to UNIFY the two reducers so
   show/list/search are truly identical. This show-vs-list divergence is a latent
   parity issue worth its own bug (see comment to follow / ask maintainer).

2. `verify_commands` in TicketState is jq-show-only (also get-verify-commands).
   The shared verify_command_entry $ref holds for show/get-verify-commands but
   NOT for list/search/native. Same caveat as #1.

3. Add `parent_status_uuid` to the TicketState inventory (Python-path field).

4. NextBatch corrections: the three "awaiting/blocked" arrays
   (skipped_blocked_story, skipped_design_awaiting, skipped_manual_awaiting) ALL
   use the key `blocked_story` (not per-variant names) -> {id,title,blocked_story}.
   Plus a MISSED top-level variant: `next-batch --limit=0` emits
   {epic_id, batch_size:0, tasks:[]} (key `tasks`, not `batch`).

5. Synthetic library/MCP shapes that are NOT raw engine JSON (the typed models
   must match what the interfaces ACTUALLY return):
   - clarity_check: library/MCP add `passed` -> {score,verdict,threshold,passed}
     (engine --json is only {score,verdict,threshold}).
   - validate: library adds `exit_code` -> 7 keys (engine --json has 6).
   - check_ac / quality_check: library returns {passed, output} — NO engine JSON
     counterpart (wraps exit code + text). Model these as GateResultOut.
   - transition/claim/reopen: library SYNTHESIZES {id,status[,assignee]} client-side
     (engine emits no such JSON). StatusResultOut is a library/MCP shape, not engine.
   - All write tools return literal "ok" (MCP) vs None (library) — return-type skew.

6. Confirmed exactly as stated: DepsGraph keys; ListDescendants (lists of id
   strings); ClarityResult; ValidateReport (6 keys); scratch envelopes; the shared
   `dep` entry {target_id,relation,link_uuid} (DepsGraph.deps IS state["deps"]);
   file_impact_entry passthrough; the {id,title} skipped_* base. ErrorEnvelope
   `error` literal = "ticket_not_found". LLM KEY_MAP is duplicated (Python
   llm_format.py + a jq mirror in ticket-lib-api.sh); `conflicts->cf` is dead.

7. BUG 1 (validate) + BUG 2 (ready) REPRODUCED empirically:
   - validate <id> -> "Unknown option: <id>", exit 1; library validate() REQUIRES
     ticket_id (can't be called id-less -> TypeError), so neither path works.
   - ready --json -> argparse exit 2 "unrecognized arguments: --json"; rebar.ready()
     raises; MCP ready_tickets ALWAYS errors. Engine ready only has --format{ids,llm}.
   - Spot-check: no OTHER read function has a flag/arg mismatch (clarity/check_ac/
     quality_check/get_*/deps/next_batch/search/list/show all verified working).
